title: JobOS.ai — Agent Job Hierarchy Schema
description: Schema for the AI-agent-centric fractal job hierarchy. Key properties: (1) Self-similar: T-3 nodes at any depth follow the same T-3 DSL. (2) Fractal: a T-3 node can have child T-3 nodes (sub-tasks) to arbitrary depth. (3) Strict DSL: every leaf must be a valid Pattern A or Pattern B T-3 constraint. (4) Machine-readable: designed for LLM agent consumption via tool APIs.

parent_agent_id.description: ID of the agent that generated this node (for agent-authored hierarchies).

t3_constraint_a.title: Pattern A — Time Minimization
t3_constraint_a.description: Minimize the time to [verb_noun] from [start_state] to [end_state], measured in [unit], target ≤ [threshold]

verb_noun.description: The action being timed — verb + direct object.

examples: CRM opportunity stage set to Qualified or Disqualified
PO approved and dispatched to supplier
user account active with first login recorded

p50.description: Median current performance (more robust than mean for skewed distributions).

t3_constraint_b.title: Pattern B — Likelihood Minimization
t3_constraint_b.description: Minimize the likelihood of [event], measured as [rate_type] in [unit], target ≤ [threshold]

examples: invoice processing error due to mismatched PO line items
contract renewal lapse due to delayed stakeholder sign-off
SLA breach on P1 ticket response
data sync failure between CRM and billing system
incorrect inventory count at cycle audit

denominator.description: Denominator population for the rate. E.g., 'per 1000 invoices processed', 'per 100 tickets created'.

agent_node.description: A single node in the fractal agent job hierarchy. All nodes at depth > 0 are T-3 constraints (Pattern A or B). The structure is self-similar: any node can decompose into child nodes following the same DSL. Leaves are atomic tool-call-ready instructions.

depth.description: Depth in the fractal tree. 0 = root mission, 1 = primary sub-tasks, 2+ = recursive decomposition.

tier_label.description: Human-readable tier label for display. E.g., 'Mission', 'Task', 'Subtask', 'Action'.

statement.description: Natural language summary of this constraint (auto-generated from the constraint fields for display).
examples: Minimize the time to qualify a lead from first contact to CRM stage update, measured in hours, target ≤ 4
Minimize the likelihood of invoice processing error due to PO mismatch, measured as error_rate in percent, target ≤ 0.5

constraint.description: Machine-readable constraint definition. Required for all non-root nodes.

agent_role.description: Which agent archetype handles this node. E.g., 'orchestrator', 'researcher', 'writer', 'validator', 'executor'.

tool_calls.description: Tool calls needed to execute this node. Only populated at leaf nodes.

success_criteria.description: Observable binary checks that confirm the constraint is met.
examples: CRM opportunity has stage = Qualified or Disqualified
Timestamp of stage update - discovery call timestamp ≤ 4 hours
No P0 invoice exceptions open for > 24h

failure_conditions.description: Observable conditions that indicate the constraint is violated and recovery is needed.

escalate_after_attempts.description: Escalate to parent agent after N failures.

children.description: Fractal decomposition: child nodes follow the same DSL. Depth N → Depth N+1. Max practical depth: 5.

crosswalk.description: Reference to corresponding managerial hierarchy node(s).

crosswalk_ref.description: Bidirectional mapping between an agent hierarchy node and managerial hierarchy node(s).

managerial_job_ids.description: IDs of corresponding T-1/T-2/T-3 nodes in the managerial hierarchy.

translation_type.description: How the agent and managerial nodes relate: direct (1:1 translation), decomposition (1 managerial → N agent), aggregation (N agent → 1 managerial), analog (similar but different framing), no_equivalent (agent-only task with no human parallel).

translation_note.description: Explanation of any non-obvious translation or framing differences.

description: Root document: a complete Agent Job Hierarchy.

agent_system.description: The AI system or agent framework this hierarchy targets. E.g., 'claude-code', 'openai-agents', 'langchain', 'custom'.

managerial_hierarchy_id.description: ID of the corresponding managerial hierarchy (for crosswalk).

mission.description: The root mission statement for this agent hierarchy. Analogous to T-1 in the managerial hierarchy but expressed as an agent objective.

statement.description: Clear, outcome-oriented mission. Should map to a T-1 managerial job.
examples: Automate the end-to-end lead qualification process to reduce conversion cycle time by 50%
Continuously monitor and minimize invoice processing errors across all procurement workflows

success_definition.description: What 'done' looks like for the agent system at a mission level.

constraints.description: Hard constraints the agent system must respect. E.g., 'never modify approved contracts', 'always notify human on >$10k decisions'.

tasks.description: Top-level tasks (depth=1) — direct decomposition of the mission into primary T-3 constraint nodes.

fractal_depth.description: Actual maximum depth reached in this hierarchy instance.

agents_required.description: List of distinct agent_role values used across all nodes.

mission.statement: Automate lead qualification to reduce conversion cycle from 72h to < 4h for all inbound SMB leads
mission.success_definition: 98% of inbound leads have CRM stage set within 4h of form submission, with qualification rationale logged
constraints: Never disqualify a lead without logging a reason code
Always notify account owner when a lead is qualified
Do not send outbound communication without human approval for deals > $50k ACV

tasks.statement: Minimize the time to enrich a lead record from CRM creation to ICP score computed, measured in minutes, target ≤ 15

constraint.end_state: ICP score computed and saved to lead record

success_criteria: Elapsed time from lead.created_at to lead.icp_score_updated_at ≤ 15 minutes
failure_conditions: Enrichment API returns no data for company

tasks.statement: Minimize the likelihood of a qualified lead going uncontacted for > 1 business day, measured as miss_rate in percent, target ≤ 2

constraint.event: qualified lead going uncontacted for > 1 business day after qualification

success_criteria: Every qualified lead has an outreach_scheduled_at within 1 business day
